title: Protein Extraction of Symbiodiniaceae from Anthopleura elegantissima
author: Virginia Weis
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.w2bfgan
materials: https://www.sigmaaldrich.com/catalog/product/sigma/p9599?lang=en®ion=US P9599 from Sigma-Aldrich (extraction from plant tissue)
1. 2% Triton X100 in FSW 2. FSW 3. Acid washed glass beads (400-600µm in diameter): https://www.sigmaaldrich.com/catalog/product/sigma/g8772?lang=en®ion=US G8772 from Sigma-Aldrich

steps.title: Obtain 25 frozen small Anthopleura elegantissima...
steps.instruction: Obtain 25 frozen small Anthopleura elegantissima (You could use 10 medium or 3 large animals also). Adjust the number of animals as needed.

steps.title: Partially defrost and cut off pedal...
steps.instruction: Partially defrost and cut off pedal discs with a razor blade (keep the algal-rich tentacle crown).

steps.title: Grind animals in mini-food processor or...
steps.instruction: Grind animals in mini-food processor or glass-teflon grinder in 30 ml FSW.

steps.title: Divide into 4-50 ml tubes, rinse processor and include rinsate
steps.instruction: Divide into 4-50 ml tubes, rinse processor and include rinsate.

steps.title: Spin for 6 min at 2500 xg at 4˚C
steps.instruction: Spin for 6 min at 2500 xg at 4˚C.

steps.title: Rinse and re-spin approximately 5 times
steps.instruction: Rinse and re-spin approximately 5 times. Vigorously resuspend pellet via vortex each time.

steps.title: Filter each tube of algae through...
steps.instruction: Filter each tube of algae through 2 layers of cheesecloth to remove large chunks of tissue

steps.title: This procedure should yield about 6...
steps.instruction: This procedure should yield about 6 ml of algal pellet for A. elegantissima .

steps.title: Work with 1
steps.instruction: Work with 1.5 ml of above algal pellet. Freeze the remainder.

steps.title: To this 1
steps.instruction: To this 1.5 ml, add 10 ml of FSW with 2% triton X100. Resuspend algae via vortex.

steps.title: Spin at 2,500 xg for 6 min at 4˚C
steps.instruction: Spin at 2,500 xg for 6 min at 4˚C. Supernatant should have greenish-yellow tint. Remove and discard supernatant.

steps.title: Rinse pellet once with 10 ml...
steps.instruction: Rinse pellet once with 10 ml of FSW with 2% triton X100 and spin again at 2,500 xg for 6 min at 4˚C.

steps.title: Remove and discard supernatant and add 3
steps.instruction: Remove and discard supernatant and add 3.75 ml of extraction buffer (with PIC). Resuspend algae and place suspension in a glass culture tube.

steps.title: Add 1-2 ml of glass beads (acid washed)
steps.instruction: Add 1-2 ml of glass beads (acid washed).

steps.title: Vortex suspension for 30 sec and...
steps.instruction: Vortex suspension for 30 sec and then place on ice for 30 sec.

steps.title: Repeat vortex and icing a total of 20 times
steps.instruction: Repeat vortex and icing a total of 20 times

steps.title: Remove suspension, away from glass beads...
steps.instruction: Remove suspension, away from glass beads and place in microfuge tubes.

steps.title: Spin at 15,000 rpm in microfuge...
steps.instruction: Spin at 15,000 rpm in microfuge for 5 min at 4˚C. Resulting supernatant should be a deep, clear orange.

steps.title: Determine protein concentration with Bradford or other protein quantification assay
steps.instruction: Determine protein concentration with Bradford or other protein quantification assay.

steps.title: The day of use, add 1...
steps.instruction: The day of use, add 1 aliquot of Protease Inhibitor Cocktail (PIC) to 10 mL of buffer. A few options for PIC:

steps.title: If you are working with an...
steps.instruction: If you are working with an algae culture, skip to step 12.
